does: DoubtProbe introduces a dual-branch defense framework operating at inference time to detect black-box jailbreak attempts against large language models. The method combines structural verification, which extracts and reconstructs structured representations of user requests to identify information-preservation failures, with semantic auditing that checks consistency across controlled transformations. Rather than relying on semantic judgment alone or attack-specific signatures, the approach frames jailbreak detection as a consistency-checking problem that exploits the observation that many jailbreaks reorganize harmful goals without removing them entirely, leaving traces detectable through structural reconstruction mismatches.
connects: This work builds on growing recognition that black-box jailbreaks often succeed through prompt restructuring and expression rewriting rather than semantic content change, extending beyond prompt-level defense approaches that typically rely on static semantic rules or known-attack patterns. It responds to limitations in existing defenses that struggle under evolving attack packaging and structure manipulation, drawing implicitly on principles from adversarial robustness and consistency-based verification methods used in other domains. The paper engages with the broader literature on LLM safety alignment, building on observations that information reorganization can evade local safety mechanisms while maintaining recoverable harmful intent during generation.
enables: DoubtProbe establishes inference-time defense as a practical avenue for protecting deployed LLMs without retraining or access to model internals, opening directions for robust detection under adaptive attacks that continuously evolve prompt structure. The consistency-checking framework could extend to other forms of adversarial prompt manipulation beyond jailbreaks, including prompt injection and indirect instruction attacks. Future work might investigate defenses that combine structural verification with model-in-the-loop auditing, develop adaptive versions that respond to evolving reorganization techniques, or integrate the approach into broader defense pipelines that combine multiple detection mechanisms.
